mcrutils 0.0.0.9013

Start or end date of the period containing each date

Source: R/period_completeness.R
period_bounds.Rd

period_start_date() and period_end_date() return the first and last calendar day of the period containing each date. They are thin, coercing, vectorized wrappers around lubridate::floor_date() and lubridate::ceiling_date() that always return a lubridate::Date (never a datetime) and validate unit.

The returned dates are always canonical calendar boundaries. Business-day calendars are used only to judge completeness (see period_is_complete()), never to compute a boundary date.

Usage

period_start_date(
  date,
  unit = "month",
  week_start = getOption("lubridate.week.start", 7)
)

period_end_date(
  date,
  unit = "month",
  week_start = getOption("lubridate.week.start", 7)
)

Arguments

date

A vector of dates (Date or coercible with lubridate::as_date()).

unit

Period size, one of "week", "month", "quarter", or "year".

week_start

Integer 1-7 giving the first day of the week (only used when unit = "week"). Defaults to getOption("lubridate.week.start", 7), so the package defers to the session's lubridate week convention.

Value

A lubridate::Date vector the same length as date.

See also

period_is_complete(), last_complete_period_end()

Examples

period_start_date(as.Date("2025-05-14"), "month") # 2025-05-01
#> [1] "2025-05-01"
period_end_date(as.Date("2025-05-14"), "month") # 2025-05-31
#> [1] "2025-05-31"
period_end_date(as.Date("2025-05-14"), "quarter") # 2025-06-30
#> [1] "2025-06-30"
